issues.title: WSLg 1.0.71: GUI session freezes, then Weston becomes unkillable in /mnt/shared_memory virtiofs
issues.next_action_reason: Missing targeted WSLg isolation details: mirrored networking/autoMemoryReclaim toggles, smaller GUI workload repro, and whether wsl --shutdown is the only recovery.
issues.suggested_reply: Hi @Mygod, thanks for the detailed diagnostics. The D-state stacks around `request_wait_answer` under `/mnt/shared_memory` and `virtio_fs_drain_queue` make this look like a WSLg shared-memory/virtiofs hang rather than a normal app deadlock. If you can reproduce or catch it again, could you add whether it also occurs with mirrored networking and autoMemoryReclaim disabled, whether a smaller GUI workload can trigger it, and whether `wsl --shutdown` is the only recovery?
